ui.title: Course Placement Assessment
ui.subtitle: A short age-appropriate assessment to place each learner into the right Python Foundation starting path.
ui.noQuestions: There are no placement questions for this course and learner group yet.

pythonFoundation.title: Python Foundation
pythonFoundation.desc: Placement assessment for learners starting Python from zero or near zero. Questions are adapted by learner group.

ready.title: Ready to start the main course
ready.desc: The learner shows enough logical thinking, basic programming vocabulary, and self-checking habits to enter the main Python Foundation roadmap.
ready.nextStep: Start Python Foundation from lesson 1, add short practice tasks after every lesson, and introduce a mini project early.

foundation.title: Start with the guided foundation path
foundation.desc: The learner understands some ideas but should move more slowly through input, variables, conditions, loops, and code reading.
foundation.nextStep: Use the slower foundation path, review each core concept with examples, and check progress every 3 to 4 lessons.

preparation.title: Strengthen thinking skills before coding
preparation.desc: The learner needs more practice with logical reasoning, step ordering, data comparison, and reading simple instructions before the main coding track.
preparation.nextStep: Assign logic warmups, flowchart exercises, input/output activities, and very short Python demos before entering the full course.

placePyStudent001.question: In a class quiz app, the student types '85' before the program calculates anything. What is this typed value called?
placePyStudent001.explain: Data provided by the user before processing is input.

placePyStudent002.question: A program displays 'Homework saved' after it finishes a task. What is the displayed message called?
placePyStudent002.explain: A message shown by a program after processing is output.

placePyStudent003.question: For a library borrowing app, which value should the program collect before it can process the book title?

options.C: The book title entered by the user

placePyStudent003.explain: A program needs the relevant user-provided data before it can process a task.

placePyStudent004.question: Which pair correctly describes input and output for a study game?

options.A: Input: final result; output: user typing
options.B: Input: game level; output: processed result
options.D: Input: no data; output: no result

placePyStudent004.explain: Input is the data given to the program; output is what the program returns or displays.

placePyStudent005.question: A small program receives the number 18 and displays double that number. What should it display?

placePyStudent006.question: A form asks for a name and a student name. Which of these are inputs?

options.D: Both the name and the student name

placePyStudent006.explain: Values entered into a form are inputs to the program.

placePyStudent007.question: Why should a program check the input in a math practice app before using it?

options.A: Programs cannot use input at all
options.C: User input may be missing, invalid, or the wrong type

placePyStudent007.explain: Programs should validate input because real user data can be incomplete or incorrect.

placePyStudent008.question: In a class quiz app, why would you use a variable such as total_points?

options.B: To stop the program from running

placePyStudent008.explain: A variable stores a value so the program can read or update it later.

placePyStudent009.question: In a homework tracker, a program runs: count = 8; count = count + 3. What is the final value of count?
placePyStudent009.explain: The second assignment adds 3 to the old value 8, so count becomes 11.

placePyStudent010.question: Which variable name is the clearest for storing a book title in a library borrowing app?

placePyStudent011.question: A program starts with total = 15. One more badge is added. Which update is most suitable?

placePyStudent012.question: In a club attendance sheet, a status variable is set to 'draft' and later set to 'submitted'. What is the final value of status?

placePyStudent013.question: A variable named is_valid stores either True or False for a science project log. What type of idea does it represent?

placePyStudent014.question: Why is it risky to reuse one variable for many unrelated meanings in a math practice app?

options.B: It makes the program harder to read and can overwrite needed values

placePyStudent014.explain: Variables should have clear meanings; reusing them for unrelated data can cause confusion and bugs.

placePyStudent015.question: A rule says a value passes when it is at least 6. If the value is 7, what should the program decide?
placePyStudent015.explain: 7 is greater than or equal to 6, so the condition is met.

placePyStudent016.question: In a homework tracker, access is allowed only when the user is active AND the payment is complete. The user is active but payment is not complete. What happens?
